title: One More
lyrics:
[verse 1]
Dad says my arms are noodles
I say they're strong like a tree
*Splat!* I hit the floor again
Why can't I do twenty-three?

[chorus]
One more! One more!
I'm not beat yet
One more! *Crash!*
I won't forget

[verse 2]
Floor's lava, hot and mean
My face is stuck in flame
I'll do a hundred—*whoa!*
Call me Push-Up King, okay?

[chorus]
One more! One more!
I'm not beat yet
One more! *Crash!*
I won't forget

[ending]
One more... *wobble*...
My arms say no...
One more... night-night...
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, piano and drums, 106 BPM, gentle bounce, warm and easy, swaying not jumping, clear warm vocal, every word easy to understand
scene: The living room carpet smells like sweat and dust at 7:47 p.m., the TV glow flickering off the half-empty water bottle and your dad’s crossed arms—he just sighed, *"Okay, last push-up, then bed,"* so you drop your chest to the floor one more time and gasp, *"One more."*
cover_description: Kid mid-push-up, face red, arms shaking like noodles, Dad’s foot tapping, clock spinning fast.
